items.headline: City Council reviews new Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan
items.summary: Mountain View is updating its plan for protecting biodiversity and managing urban trees. This study session on April 28 will shape how the city approaches green space and environmental goals going forward.

items.headline: New mixed-use development proposed: 67 homes and retail near Speedy's Tacos
items.summary: A 4.22-acre site in Sunnyvale could be redeveloped with 55 townhomes, 12 apartments, and a new two-story retail building while preserving the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The Planning Commission held a study session on April 27 to review the project's design and feasibility.

items.headline: Campbell raises business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council approved an ordinance revising business license tax rates in the municipal code, effective in the new fiscal year. This annual update affects local businesses operating in Campbell.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: City approves temporary supergraphic signs near Levi's Stadium
items.summary: Santa Clara authorized temporary large-scale advertising signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. This marks a notable exception to normal signage rules for the special event period.

items.headline: Milpitas updates development fees and user charges for FY 2026-27
items.summary: The city council approved changes to development fees and various user charges that will affect costs for new construction projects and city services. Residents and developers should be aware these adjustments may impact housing affordability and project timelines.

items.headline: Santa Clara County presents homelessness action plan to Milpitas
items.summary: County officials presented an update on the Community Plan to End Homelessness, which affects how Milpitas coordinates services for unhoused residents. This regional initiative will shape local homeless services and shelter policies going forward.

items.headline: City Council hosts guide to building permits and timelines in San José
items.summary: The council presented a resource explaining the development process from initial planning through occupancy, covering permitting requirements and project timelines. This could help residents and developers better understand how long projects take and what's involved in building in the city.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates climate action plan toward 2028 goals
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission reviewed progress on the Climate Action Playbook with a new Game Plan targeting FY 2025-26 completion. This affects what environmental initiatives the city will prioritize in coming years.
